title: Thoseghar Waterfalls
description: Thoseghar waterfalls is a scenic spot located near the small village of Thoseghar, 20 km from Satara city, at the edge of the Konkan region, in Western India. There are a series of waterfalls, some of them 15 to 20 meters and one of 200 meters in height.

title: Devkund Waterfall
description: Devkund Waterfall is a waterfall located in Bhira, near Raigad district, Maharashtra, India. It is a 'plunge' waterfall pouring massively, as a scenic combination of three waterfalls and is said to be the origin of Kundalika river.

title: Randha Falls
description: Randha falls is situated in the Ahmednagar district of Maharashtra. The clear water of the Pravara River fiercely falls from a height of 170 feet into a massive gorge. It is considered one of the prominent waterfalls in India.
